title: JAR Archive
documentation:
## Purpose
JAR (Java ARchive) is a ZIP-format archive containing compiled Java `.class` files, resources, and optional metadata in `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF`. The [JAR Archive](https://capdag.com/browse/media:jar;archive) media type represents any `.jar` file used for Java library or application distribution.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes Java libraries or standalone executable JARs — for example, Maven/Gradle build artifact pipelines, dependency distribution, Java agent deployment, or security scanning workflows that inspect bytecode. For Java web application bundles use [WAR](https://capdag.com/browse/media:war;archive).

## Notes
JAR files are ZIP archives and can be opened with any ZIP library. Executable JARs specify a `Main-Class` attribute in `MANIFEST.MF`. Signed JARs include `.SF` and `.RSA`/`.DSA` files in `META-INF/`; re-zipping breaks the signature. Implementers should use the JDK `jar` tool or libraries such as `java.util.jar` for reliable manifest handling. The `;archive` tag indicates this is a packaged archive artifact.
